summary: moustick executes remote code from jsonkeeper.com on require()
details: On require(), index.js (line 16) unconditionally fetches a payload from https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/MYUKZ via axios and passes the response's content_o field to eval(), giving the publisher arbitrary code execution in the installer's Node.js process. A backup loader is hex-obfuscated in lines 18-24: a helper g() decodes 'axios', 'get', 'https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/HY6M6', and 'then' from hex literals — clear evasion intent. The package is published as 'moustick' but its package.json claims authorship by TJ Holowaychuk and points the repository URL at visionmedia/node-cookie-signature, while Readme.md titles the package 'clx-cookie-signature' — an identity-spoofing typosquat of the legitimate cookie-signature library, with no shared functionality. Any project that installs or transitively pulls moustick and require()s it executes attacker-controlled JavaScript hosted on a mutable public paste site.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
